response.content: Avatar: Fire and Ash - Wikipedia Jump to content From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia "Avatar 3" and "Fire and Ash" redirect here. For the third season of Avatar: The Last Airbender , see Avatar: The Last Airbender season 3 . For other uses, see Fire and Ash (disambiguation) . 2025 film by James Cameron Avatar: Fire and Ash Theatrical release poster Directed by James Cameron Screenplay by James Cameron Rick Jaffa Amanda Silver Story by James Cameron Rick Jaffa Amanda Silver Josh Friedman Shane Salerno Produced by James Cameron Jon Landau Starring Sam Worthington Zoe Saldaña Sigourney Weaver Stephen Lang Kate Winslet Cinematography Russell Carpenter Edited by Stephen E. Rivkin [ a ] Nicolas de Toth John Refoua Jason Gaudio James Cameron David Brenner [ 1 ] Music by Simon Franglen [ b ] Production company Lightstorm Entertainment Distributed by 20th Century Studios Release dates December 1, 2025 ( 2025-12-01 ) ( Dolby Theatre ) [ 2 ] December 19, 2025 ( 2025-12-19 ) (United States) Running time 197 minutes [ 3 ] Country United States Language English Budget $350–400 million [ c ] Box office $1.490 billion [ 8 ] [ 9 ] Avatar: Fire and Ash is a 2025 American epic science fiction film directed by James Cameron from a screenplay he co-wrote with Rick Jaffa and Amanda Silver . Produced by Lightstorm Entertainment , it is the third installment in the Avatar film series and the sequel to Avatar: The Way of Water (2022). The film features Sam Worthington , Zoe Saldaña , Sigourney Weaver , Stephen Lang , and Kate Winslet reprising their roles from previous films. The story follows the human-turned- Na'vi Jake Sully and his family on the habitable moon Pandora , as they face the combined threat of the human RDA forces and the Mangkwan, a ruthless Na'vi clan. Following the box office success of Avatar (2009), Cameron announced two sequels, with Avatar 3 aiming for a December 2015 release. Movie Audience Score 76 Review Score 79 Starring Sam Worthington Zoe Saldaña Sigourney Weaver Director James Cameron Watch on Amazon Video Avatar: Fire and Ash Highlights PG-13 3 hr 18 min Dec 19th, 2025 Adventure , Fantasy , Science Fiction Part of Avatar Collection Facebook X Reddit Share Embed Movie Copied i Plot In the wake of the devastating war against the RDA and the loss of their eldest son, Jake Sully and Neytiri face a new threat on Pandora: the Ash People, a violent and power-hungry Na'vi tribe led by the ruthless Varang. Jake's family must fight for their survival and the future of Pandora in a conflict that pushes them to their emotional and physical limits. Movie Details Where to Watch Videos Highlights Quotes Full Cast & Crew News Where to Watch Avatar: Fire and Ash powered by US UK CA AU TR FR DE IT NL IN BR UAE Amazon Video 4K Buy $19.99 Apple TV Store 4K Buy $19.99 Google Play Movies Buy $19.99 YouTube Buy $19.99 Avatar: Fire and Ash Review 'Avatar: Fire and Ash' is a Visually Stunning One-Time Theatrical Experience. Breathtaking visuals make it a must-see on the big screen, but the lack of plot and character development, keep it from deserving repeat viewings at home. The movie is meant to be seen in 3D on the biggest screen possible surrounded by an audience, and in that way, ‘Fire and Ash’ is a huge triumph, and another cinematic extravaganza from the “King of the World” James Cameron. But, with vague characters that never grow or change, a limited plot and almost no real story to tell, I fear the movie will not hold up in repeat viewings, especially on smaller home screens, with no real substance outside of the visual spectacle. response.snippet: Avatar: Fire and Ash is the epic third movie in James Cameron's groundbreaking sci-fi saga, arriving in theaters December 19, 2025. Continuing the story on Pandora, the film pits Jake Sully and Neytiri's family against the Ash People—a powerful new Na'vi clan—leading to an intense clash that will change the course of the franchise.
response.content: Avatar: Fire and Ash Movie – Release Date, Plot, Cast, Where to Watch, Trailer & Everything We Know - High On Films Skip to content Share it Avatar: Fire and Ash is the epic third movie in James Cameron’s groundbreaking sci-fi saga, arriving in theaters December 19, 2025. Continuing the story on Pandora, the film pits Jake Sully and Neytiri’s family against the Ash People—a powerful new Na’vi clan—leading to an intense clash that will change the course of the franchise. What is the movie ‘Avatar: Fire and Ash’ about?: Plot Details Set after the events of Avatar: The Way of Water , Jake and Neytiri’s family mourns the loss of their son Neteyam as internal and external threats intensify. The mysterious Ash People, led by Varang (Oona Chaplin), surface as a fiery and militant faction who side with the resurrected Miles Quaritch against the Sully family and Metkayina clan. The plot centers on the struggle to defend Pandora and its tribes, the moral dilemmas of war, and the looming possibility of greater human intervention—now that certain humans can survive on Pandora without masks. The narrative promises fresh perspectives on Na’vi culture and environmental conflict, as Jake, Neytiri, and their children confront spiritual tests, family drama, and volatile new enemies in a visually breathtaking world. Cast Sam Worthington as Jake Sully Zoe Saldaña as Neytiri Sigourney Weaver as Kiri Stephen Lang as Miles Quaritch Kate Winslet as Ronal Oona Chaplin as Varang (Ash People leader) Cliff Curtis as Tonowari Joel David Moore as Norm Spellman CCH Pounder as Mo’at Edie Falco as General Ardmore David Thewlis, Jemaine Clement, Giovanni Ribisi, Britain Dalton, Trinity Jo-Li Bliss, Jack Champion, Brendan Cowell, Bailey Bass, Filip Geljo, Duane Evans Jr. Michelle Yeoh as Dr. Karina Mogue View this post on Instagram A post shared by Zoe Saldaña (@zoesaldana) Production James Cameron directs, co-writes, and produces the film, building on a story crafted with Rick Jaffa, Amanda Silver,
